title: Indian Economic Services Officer
description: Indian Economic Service (IES) officers are economists who advise the Government of India on economic policy matters. They play a crucial role in formulating and implementing economic development plans, analyzing economic trends, and contributing to policy decisions across various sectors.
content: IES officers are recruited through a competitive examination conducted by the UPSC. They work in various ministries and departments of the Government of India, including the Ministry of Finance, NITI Aayog, and the Reserve Bank of India. Their responsibilities include economic forecasting, policy analysis, project evaluation, and advising on macroeconomic issues.

financialFeasibility.note: While the UPSC exam is accessible to all, coaching and resources can be expensive. Students from middle-class backgrounds can succeed with dedication and access to affordable study materials.

sectorScore.remarks: IES officers primarily work in the government sector. Opportunities in the private sector are limited but may exist in consulting firms or research institutions.

relatedCareers.reason: Shares core skills in economic analysis and policy formulation.
relatedCareers.title: Economist

relatedCareers.reason: Involves working for the government and contributing to public service.
relatedCareers.title: Civil Servant

relatedCareers.reason: Provides data analysis and insights for economic decision-making.
relatedCareers.title: Statistician

genderDistribution.note: While traditionally male-dominated, there is increasing representation of women in the IES.
